title: Add SKAnimatedSurfaceView for Blazor with runtime canvas/GL backend switching
body:
Extracts the animated surface view concept into its own focused PR, introducing `SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Blazor` — a new Blazor library with `SKAnimatedSurfaceView`, the Blazor equivalent of the MAUI `SKAnimatedSurfaceView` + `PART_DrawingSurface` control-template pattern.

## New library: `SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Blazor`

- **`SKAnimatedSurfaceView`** — ~60fps `PeriodicTimer` render loop component that accepts a `UseGL` parameter to switch between `SKCanvasView` (software) and `SKGLView` (WebGL/GPU) at runtime. The `OnPaintSurface` callback is unified across both backends (`Action<SKSurface, SKSize>`), so drawing code requires no changes when toggling backends.

```razor
<SKAnimatedSurfaceView UseGL="@useGL"
                       IsAnimationEnabled="@isPlaying"
                       OnUpdate="HandleUpdate"
                       OnPaintSurface="HandlePaint"
                       style="width: 400px; height: 400px;" />
```

- Subclass and override `UpdateAsync(TimeSpan)` for reusable animated components; `OnUpdate`/`OnPaintSurface` callbacks cover the delegate use-case without subclassing.
- Cleans up the `PeriodicTimer` loop via `IAsyncDisposable`; loop auto-restarts after a `UseGL` toggle once the new DOM element is ready.

## Blazor sample

New `/animatedsurface` demo page showing runtime canvas↔GL backend switching, pause/resume, and speed control:

![Animated Surface Demo](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c9b7ab1e-8eb5-4618-bdbd-0ef5859b9682)

## Other

- `tests/SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Blazor.Tests/` — unit tests for default params, `IsAnimationEnabled` toggle, `UseGL` toggle, and `OnUpdate` callback
- `docs/docs/animated-surface-blazor.md` — API reference and usage guide
- Solution and sample project references updated

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> Can you look at https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp.Extended/pull/365 and extract the animated surface view into a separate pr wit code, samples, tests and docs.
> 
> Have a look at ways to improve and fix the bugs. 
> 
> One feature we need to add is a way to swap out the canvas view with a gl view in the razor. This needs to be a feature on the sample so the animated surface view should handle both. It would be up to the sample/consumer to decide what underlying view. 
> 
> This is sort of what the Maui app did with the control template. 


</details>
